items.headline: City takes action on affordability protections for subsidized housing
items.summary: The City Council addressed how to maintain affordability restrictions on housing when project-based subsidies expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects residents living in affordable housing developments and ensures protections remain in place even if funding or ownership changes.

items.headline: Storm sewer system master plan moving forward with consultant
items.summary: The City amended its agreement with Wood Rodgers, Inc. to continue city-wide master planning for the storm sewer system. This planning effort will guide future improvements to how San José handles stormwater drainage and flooding prevention.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 Development Underway on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four new homes permitted simultaneously in the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 subdivision along Altino Boulevard (lots 19-23), ranging from $487K-$573K. This represents a significant cluster of new residential construction in a single neighborhood phase.

items.headline: Sherly Place Housing Development Approved with Three New Homes
items.summary: Three new homes permitted at Sherly Place (lots 2-4) in the $496K-$570K range. This appears to be another coordinated residential development adding housing supply to the area.

items.headline: City Council Reviews FY 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to examine Mountain View's capital improvement projects for fiscal year 2026-27, which typically include infrastructure, facilities, and development investments. This gives residents insight into what major projects the city is planning to fund in the coming year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced changes to Chapter 19.71 of the municipal code governing residential tenant protections. The specific amendments were not detailed in the agenda, but this ordinance affects renter rights and landlord obligations in the city.
